content.text:
Looking at this carefully. Baseline shows BRAM18=0 with LUT=84026, FF=13258. The 1024×8 `in_latch` array is written 32 entries at a time (one tile per beat), so Vivado implements it as ~8000 FFs plus a 1024:1 async read mux — eating LUTs and FFs both.

My plan: reorganize `in_latch` into a 32-entry × 256-bit memory written one row per beat with synchronous registered read. This naturally infers ~8 BRAM18s (256-bit wide port needs 8 parallel BRAM18s), drops ~8000 FFs, and removes the 1024:1 LUT mux. The MAC pipeline timing stays identical because the registered BRAM read happens in parallel with the existing registered weight read.
